summary: Here’s a rough, parameter-driven OpenSCAD starter for the “Twin Wake” part. I’ve kept the geometry simple and support-friendly: a compact base, two rising ribbon arms, and soft transitions rather than aggressive cantilevers. The ribbon thickness is held at 3 mm minimum, with room to tune the silhouette later.
parameters: Twin ribbon start zone centered on the base
Each arm kept at 3 mm thickness, 14 mm visual width
Gentle offset and twist only, to avoid support-heavy undersides
fit_checks: Confirm hole spacing on the Twin Wake.
Check fastener clearance and wrench access.
Verify drain path does not weaken the load path.
export_detail: Exported CadQuery custom-form assets as STL, STEP, and 3MF, then assembled a slicer pack for creality-k2-pro-combo using the creative-concept-balanced profile.
